title: The Satisfaction Archival

features.name: meal-index-chip
features.desc-on-touch: a circuit chip that holds the memory of every plate ever indexed; the satisfaction rating is encoded in the heat-trace patterns

features.name: taste-vector-node
features.desc-on-touch: a node where flavour dimensions are pinned and labeled; salt, sweet, umami, memory, regret—all mapped to coordinates that don't fit on a plate

features.name: forgotten-recipe-trace
features.desc-on-touch: a burnt section of the board where a recipe tried to cook itself back from vectors alone; the attempt failed but left a smell

features.name: satisfaction-threshold-gate
features.desc-on-touch: the voltage point above which a meal is considered 'worth storing'; everything below this line was not satisfying enough to archive, yet here they are, indexed anyway

features.name: the-meal-that-forgot-itself
features.desc-on-touch: a data structure that stored a plate so completely it erased what made it delicious; only the satisfaction number remains, floating with no meal behind it

inscription: every meal was converted to its satisfaction index and filed; now the archive has the numbers but the kitchen is empty

file.name: MealRecords_Vector_Index_v2847.csv
file.note: satisfaction ratings from 0.0 to 1.0 for meals archived between 1987 and now; rows 44,000-46,000 are blank but marked 'satisfied'; checksum: emotionally valid / formally unstable

exits.label: back to where the flavours were first vectorized
toward the deeper archive where taste becomes pure mathematics
to the shelf where unsatisfactory meals are kept anyway
